title: Real-world 7-day Kasett compaction analysis

sample.note_duplicate: 64734813-1063-4693-96d6-d7b994fd9ba8.jsonl.kasett-meta.jsonl is byte-identical to agent:main:telegram:group:-1003723465246:topic:12388.jsonl.kasett-meta.jsonl (legacy filename → same session). Effective unique compactions = 9.
sample.note_recovered: Two of the three multi-compaction sessions are the v1 + v3-recovered backfill pair (64734813 and its dup alias). Same ts, same content — recovered is a retroactive schema upgrade, not a real second compaction. Effective real multi-compaction session count = 1 (4ce855d8).

sub_thread_count.cap_hit: ALL v3 records have exactly 5 sub-threads — schema cap of 5 confirmed reached on every v3 emission

schema_observation.expected_field_key_state: Per spec, v3 should expose `key_state` as a flat structured array.
schema_observation.actual_field: Sidecars contain `key_state_candidates` (the un-filtered candidate dump) but NOT a curated `key_state` field. The curated key_state appears ONLY inside the trailing `## Exact identifiers` JSON block of `summary_rich`, not as a top-level sidecar field.
schema_observation.implication: Programmatic consumers must parse summary_rich to extract key_state. The 'sidecar enables structured access' design intent is partially unrealized.

vanilla_kasett_ablation.design: On the one real multi-compaction session (4ce855d8 C2), we constructed VANILLA = summary_rich with the trailing '## Exact identifiers' JSON block stripped (3,559 chars of prose only), and KASETT = full summary_rich (8,340 chars including thread_meta_v2 + key_state JSON). This is the correct ablation given that v3 inlines the structured block INTO summary_rich rather than keeping it as a separate consumer-side field.
vanilla_kasett_ablation.statistical_warning: n=7 probes from n=1 real multi-compaction session. Underpowered for population-level inference. McNemar test on 5 discordant pairs (kasett wins all 5) gives a one-sided exact p = 1/2^5 = 0.03125, suggestive but NOT robust given single-session sourcing.

per_session_breakdown.note: v3-recovered is a backfill of v1 — same timestamp range, no real second compaction event

bugs_observed.v1_to_v3_partial_adoption: Of 6 v1 records, 3 are from 2026-05-19 (today) — Path C compaction (compact-CNsgTXwX.js) is still producing v1 schema after the v2 hot-patch. Confirms bug from 2026-05-19-sidecar-failure-diagnostic.md.
bugs_observed.missed_compaction_2026_05_18T21:19: Known compaction event on session 5ab439f7-...-topic-5392 produced no sidecar at all. Not present in /home/node/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/. Confirms the v3-path-c.diff is not yet in production.
bugs_observed.sub_thread_cap_5: Every v3 record reports exactly 5 sub-threads — hitting (or being capped at) the schema ceiling. Suggests truncation; real thread diversity may be higher.
bugs_observed.key_state_field_missing: Top-level `key_state` field absent; only `key_state_candidates` present (containing raw URL/path mentions, not curated identifiers). The curated key_state lives only inside the summary_rich JSON block.
bugs_observed.duplicate_sidecar_files: agent:main:telegram:group:-1003723465246:topic:12388.jsonl.kasett-meta.jsonl is a byte-identical copy of 64734813's sidecar — orphaned legacy filename. Cleanup opportunity.

verdict_detail: On the single real multi-compaction case in the 7-day window, kasett's structured block recovers 5 high-value identifiers that vanilla prose drops (71.4 pp absolute lift). The mechanism works as designed. However n=1 session × 7 probes is far below publication threshold. Phase 4 (synthetic, 22.2% vs 55.6%, p=0.0005) remains the publishable result; Phase H confirms the mechanism produces the SAME class of wins on a real Clyde session.
